steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Text 1 concludes that The Nutcracker should no longer be produced because it is outdated. The question asks how Text 2 responds to that view.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — By questioning the idea that the story of The Nutcracker is stuck in the past and by rejecting the suggestion that contemporary audiences would enjoy an updated version.: Text 2 concedes the show is outdated but explicitly supports updating it — the opposite of rejecting updates. ✗
  • Choice B — By agreeing that contemporary audiences have largely stopped going to see performances of The Nutcracker because it's so old-fashioned.: Text 2 describes The Nutcracker as a “holiday favorite” still drawing audiences, not one they've abandoned. ✗
  • Choice C — By pointing out that most dance companies could increase their incomes by offering modernized versions of The Nutcracker.: Text 2 never claims modernization would boost income for most companies; that generalization does not appear. ✗
  • Choice D — By suggesting that dance companies should consider offering revised versions of The Nutcracker instead of completely rejecting the show.: Text 2 grants the show is dated yet opposes Text 1's call to discontinue it, citing Alicia Carter's Hot Chocolate Nutcracker as proof that creative revision works. ✓
That is the correct answer.
